brand.name: Android Car Apps Installer
brand.title: Install cool apps on your gcar 7xx
brand.description: This application helps you install apps on your car, and also sets their permissions, you can see which apps below.
brand.supportNote: If you want to add an app that is not listed here please contact support IT team.

requirements: Press button How to enable ADB on your car first.
Use Chrome or Edge on a computer, or Chrome on an Android phone with a working OTG adapter.
Connect through the driver-side upper USB-A port with a data cable and OTG adapter.
After ADB is enabled, tap Connect device, select the vehicle in Chrome’s USB picker, and approve USB debugging on the head unit if prompted.

steps.title: Authorize the head unit
steps.description: Open the WebUSB picker, choose the car, and let the site establish an ADB session in-browser.

steps.title: Review the public payload
steps.description: Open the public script and package plan before you run anything.

steps.title: Execute sequentially
steps.description: Run commands and APK installs in order, with terminal-style output and a completion popup once the pipeline finishes.

guide.title: How to enable ADB
guide.summary: Use the trim-specific time, date, and encryption code below, then return here and connect from Chrome.
prerequisites: Car must be on and the front driver-side USB ports should be illuminated.
Use the driver-side upper USB-A port only.
A USB-A OTG adapter is required or ADB will not initialize correctly.
Use a proper data cable, not a charge-only cable.

steps.title: 1. Prepare the connection and set the system time
bullets: Connect the laptop to the driver-side upper USB-A port.
Use a USB-C to USB-A data cable together with a USB-A OTG adapter.
Set the head unit date and time using the trim-specific values shown above.

steps.title: 2. Open the hidden engineering menu
bullets: Open the phone dialer on the main head unit screen.
Enter the code *#20240730#*.
steps.note: This opens the hidden engineering menu.

steps.title: 3. Open the encryption settings
bullets: Tap 加密设置 in the left-hand menu.
Press the blue button labelled 进入加密设置.

steps.title: 4. Enter the trim-specific encryption code
bullets: Comfort / Luxury: 353000
Flagship: 809877
If the code fails, return to the dialer step and complete steps 2 to 4 within 60 seconds.

steps.title: 5. Toggle ADB on
bullets: Enable ADB in engineering settings.
When ADB turns on, the light on the driver-side USB port will switch off.
If your cable and OTG are correct, the light should illuminate again once the computer is properly connected.

steps.title: 6. Return here and connect from Chrome
bullets: After ADB is enabled, you can reset the system time and date to the current values.
You can leave ADB enabled; there is no need to switch it off again.
Open this page in Chrome or Edge on a computer, or Chrome on an Android phone using OTG.
Tap Connect device, select the vehicle in Chrome’s USB dialog, then accept the USB debugging prompt on the head unit if it appears.
Run the installer once the terminal shows that the bridge is ready.

execution.scriptDescription: This script is loaded from the public content repo so users can inspect it before running anything.
execution.completionMessage: The live package pipeline completed successfully.

pipeline.label: Remove legacy package
pipeline.description: Remove the older package if it is already installed.

pipeline.label: Install Clock & Weather
pipeline.description: Download and install the Clock & Weather APK from the public GitHub source.

pipeline.label: Grant app permissions
pipeline.description: Grant the runtime permissions required by the installed app.
pipeline.command:
pm grant com.g700.clockweather android.permission.ACCESS_COARSE_LOCATION 2>/dev/null || true
pm grant com.g700.clockweather android.permission.ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION 2>/dev/null || true
pm grant com.g700.clockweather android.permission.POST_NOTIFICATIONS 2>/dev/null || true
pm grant com.g700.clockweather android.permission.ACCESS_BACKGROUND_LOCATION 2>/dev/null || true

pipeline.label: Set app operations
pipeline.description: Apply app-ops permissions and background whitelist settings.
pipeline.command:
appops set com.g700.clockweather ACCESS_BACKGROUND_LOCATION allow 2>/dev/null || true
appops set com.g700.clockweather POST_NOTIFICATION allow 2>/dev/null || true
appops set com.g700.clockweather REQUEST_INSTALL_PACKAGES allow 2>/dev/null || true
appops set com.g700.clockweather SYSTEM_ALERT_WINDOW allow 2>/dev/null || true
dumpsys deviceidle whitelist +com.g700.clockweather 2>/dev/null || true

pipeline.label: Launch the app
pipeline.description: Open the installed app once setup is complete.

pipeline.label: Verify installation
pipeline.description: Check that the package is installed and visible on the device.
pipeline.command:
echo "[package]"
pm list packages | grep -F "com.g700.clockweather" || echo "NOT INSTALLED"

pipeline.label: Verify permissions and appops
pipeline.description: Print the package permissions and app-ops state for review.
pipeline.command:
echo "[requested/granted permissions]"
dumpsys package com.g700.clockweather | grep -E "android.permission.(ACCESS_COARSE_LOCATION|ACCESS_FINE_LOCATION|ACCESS_BACKGROUND_LOCATION|POST_NOTIFICATIONS|REQUEST_INSTALL_PACKAGES|REQUEST_IGNORE_BATTERY_OPTIMIZATIONS)|android.car.permission.CAR_EXTERIOR_ENVIRONMENT" || true
echo "[appops]"
appops get com.g700.clockweather 2>/dev/null | grep -E "ACCESS_BACKGROUND_LOCATION|POST_NOTIFICATION|REQUEST_INSTALL_PACKAGES|SYSTEM_ALERT_WINDOW" || echo "No matching appops shown"

pipeline.label: Verify runtime state
pipeline.description: Check launcher entries, running process, and service state.
pipeline.command:
echo "[receiver/service]"
dumpsys package com.g700.clockweather | grep -E "BootReceiver|AutomationForegroundService|MAIN|LAUNCHER" || true
echo "[process]"
pidof com.g700.clockweather || echo "Process not running"
echo "[activity/service snapshot]"
dumpsys activity services | grep -A 20 -B 5 "com.g700.clockweather" || true

legal.title: Read before installing
legal.summary: Running custom software on a vehicle head unit is inherently risky.
bullets: You accept responsibility for any system instability, crashes, or unexpected vehicle behavior.
You accept responsibility for any warranty impact, service refusal, or data loss.
This tool is nothing to do with any vehicle manufacturer, agent, reseller or affiliate
Only continue if you are comfortable using developer-style tools on the car.
